channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ACM staff informed of chair Antony Catalano’s criminal charges through news reports
item.description: Australian Community Media (ACM) staff, including managing director Tony Kendall, were caught off guard when they learned that the company’s chair and major investor, Antony Catalano, faced criminal c...

item.title: How Outdoor Wellness Architecture is Transforming Health and Living Spaces
item.description: Outdoor wellness architecture is emerging as a significant shift in how people approach health and well-being, moving away from traditional indoor gyms toward nature-integrated spaces. Initially popul...

item.title: African Governments Cautious Over Citizens Recruited by Russian Forces
item.description: Several African countries are handling carefully the issue of their citizens being recruited into Russian military forces. Kenya’s foreign minister, Musalia Mudavadi, is visiting Russia amid rising pr...

item.title: Commuters and advocacy groups stage protest against rising oil prices and fare increases
item.description: Various commuter groups and advocacy organizations gathered in Metro Manila on March 17, 2026, to protest against the rising oil prices and the corresponding increase in public transportation fares. P...

item.title: Middle East Crude Benchmarks Surge to Records Amid US-Israel-Iran War Supply Disruptions
item.description: Spot premiums for Middle East crude grades like Oman and Dubai have soared to all-time highs due to the ongoing US and Israeli war on Iran, which has severely disrupted supply and trade. Cash Dubai’s ...

item.title: Chike Claims He Was Scammed of $20,000 by Social Media User
item.description: Popular Nigerian singer Chike Ezekpeazu don raise alarm say one social media user wey dem dey call Chioma Nneoma Ochibili don scam am of $20,000. Chike yan say he give the money to Chioma for one busi...

item.title: Iran presses India to release three seized tankers amid talks on safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: According to the report, Iran has asked India to release three tankers seized near Indian waters in February as part of wider talks on securing safe passage for Indian‑flagged or India‑bound vessels t...

item.title: Investigation into Suspect in Nyeri Women's Double Murder
item.description: Police in Nyeri County are investigating a man suspected of murdering two women in a shocking double homicide that has left the community reeling. The suspect, who was the last person seen with the vi...

item.title: Mmeli expresses support for Thandeka ahead of Big Brother Mzansi finale
item.description: Big Brother Mzansi evictee Mmeli Khumalo has expressed his unwavering support for Thandeka, his 'LOML' (love of my life), as she heads into the Season 6 finale. In an interview following his eviction,...

item.title: Trump criticises Gavin Newsom over dyslexia and presidential suitability
item.description: President Donald Trump has once again targeted California Governor Gavin Newsom over his dyslexia, suggesting that the learning disability should disqualify Newsom from the presidency. Speaking to rep...

item.title: SA Couple Arrested in Florida for Alleged Grocery Theft
item.description: South African reality TV personalities Melany “Mel” Viljoen and her husband Peet Viljoen, known from The Real Housewives of Pretoria, were arrested in Boca Raton, Florida, on March 10, 2026, for alleg...

item.title: Documentary Exposes the Harmful Impact of Social Media Platforms
item.description: Inside the Rage Machine, presented by BBC correspondent Marianna Spring, examines how social media platforms like Meta and X operate in ways that prioritise profit over human wellbeing. Through interv...

item.title: Remedies for Left-Behind Parents in International Child Abduction under Philippine Law
item.description: The Philippines, as one of over 50 signatories to the Hague Abduction Convention (HAC) ratified in 2016, now offers stronger remedies for left-behind parents when a child is wrongfully removed or reta...

item.title: Sowore Explains Why Nnamdi Kanu Refused Conditions Accepted by Sunday Igboho
item.description: For Abuja, former presidential candidate Omoyele Sowore yarn say the main reason why Nnamdi Kanu dey still dey locked up na because e refuse accept some conditions wey Yoruba activist Sunday Igboho do...

item.title: Israel launches limited ground operation in southern Lebanon amid escalating conflict with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel has intensified its military presence in southern Lebanon, launching a limited ground operation against Hezbollah strongholds following two weeks of heavy fighting. The conflict escalated after...

item.title: Average Household Food Basket Costs R5,422 for Seven-Person Families in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, many households are managing the challenge of feeding large families on limited incomes. The Household Affordability Index, which tracks the cost of a typical household food basket fo...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Raises Concern with Election Commission Over Officer Transfers
item.description: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has formally approached the Election Commission expressing concern over the removal of certain government officers. She argued that the sudden transfers and removals of ...

item.title: Canada’s Einarson Team Maintains Undefeated Streak at Women’s World Curling Championship
item.description: Kerri Einarson’s Canadian curling team remains undefeated at the Women’s World Curling Championship after defeating Denmark 9-6 in a challenging match. Representing Gimli Curling Club in Manitoba, Ein...

item.title: Basilan Records Highest Prevalence of Child Stunting in the Philippines
item.description: According to the Second Congressional Commission on Education (EDCOM II), Basilan has the highest child stunting rate in the Philippines. Based on data from Operation Timbang Plus as of March 25, 2025...

item.title: Canada prioritizes grassroots sport and accessibility amid funding discussions
item.description: Adam van Koeverden, Canada’s secretary of state for sport, recently returned from the Milan Cortina Winter Games and the Arctic Winter Games in Whitehorse, highlighting the federal government’s contin...
